verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1) is canonically a cytosolic/nuclear stress-induced chaperone with no TM domain or classical membrane anchor. However, it is one of the best-documented examples of a stress-induced / cancer-cell-restricted ecto-chaperone: (1) heat shock, oxidative stress, and other cellular stresses induce rapid translocation of HSP70 to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it is detectable by flow cytometry on intact, non-permeabilized cells; (2) tumor cells constitutively display surface HSP70 on their outer leaflet (ecto-HSP70), a phenomenon extensively validated by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry — and this has motivated multiple clinical programs (e.g., antibody-based and NK-cell-based therapeutics specifically targeting cell-surface HSP70 on tumor cells). The membrane association involves lipid-binding (particularly phosphatidylserine and ceramide) at the outer leaflet, which, while not a classical TM anchor, is documented as wash-resistant in the cancer/stress context. This firmly places HSPA1A in contextual / cell_state_induced.
